artifacts.title: OpenLAG 0.4.0 Release Baseline
artifacts.description: Published baseline containing project-local artifact contracts, relation runtime payloads, richer lint report data, dynamic portal contract metadata, and init-all support.
artifacts.markdownBody:
# OpenLAG 0.4.0 Release Baseline

## Baseline Scope

VER-040 is the audited stable baseline before 0.5.0 lifecycle expansion. It provides valid contracts, relation payload generation, lint reporting, and portal metadata, but lifecycle traceability remains mostly implicit.

## Repository Boundary State

In this baseline, repository-root `docs/*` was still the observable OpenLAG project, but branch/version evolution details were not deeply modeled in source artifacts. Readers often needed branch comparison to understand exact deltas.

## Behavioral Baseline

- Contract loading and graph generation worked from root documentation.
- Validation checks (`check --profile`) enforced release and develop constraints.
- Generated outputs were deterministic for the same source state.

## Gap Closed By VER-050

What VER-040 lacks is explicit lifecycle navigation across change rationale, affected modules, migration implications, and governance evidence linkages. VER-050 addresses that gap by adding denser cross-artifact traceability.

raw.title: OpenLAG 0.4.0 Release Baseline
raw.description: Published baseline containing project-local artifact contracts, relation runtime payloads, richer lint report data, dynamic portal contract metadata, and init-all support.

artifacts.title: OpenLAG 0.5.0 Lifecycle Evolution
artifacts.description: Contract-driven lifecycle evolution adding starter onboarding, freeze/export, rule contracts, profile packs, preview workflow, and self-documenting release evidence.
artifacts.markdownBody:
# OpenLAG 0.5.0 Lifecycle Evolution

## Evolution Summary

VER-050 turns OpenLAG into an explicitly self-documented release lifecycle. The key difference from VER-040 is not only feature presence but traceability quality: 0.5.0 describes concrete changes, ownership, verification, and governance impact in first-class artifacts.

## Repository Boundary Change

The repository root is now the authoritative OpenLAG project boundary for lifecycle reporting. `docs/*` is the primary evidence source, while `internal/dev-sandbox/docs` is explicitly secondary and used for compatibility and regression checks.

## Branch And Workflow Change

- `main` remains the published 0.4.0 baseline reference.
- `develop` carries 0.5.0 lifecycle evolution and release-candidate validation.
- Freeze generation from root docs is treated as release evidence, not optional narrative output.

## Implementation Impact Areas

- CLI flows in `scripts/cli`: check/generate/freeze workflows tightened around lifecycle evidence generation.
- Core generation and freeze logic in `scripts/core`: deterministic transformation from source docs to runtime payloads and documentary exports.
- Contracts/profiles in `docs/contracts` and `profiles`: explicit governance and export behavior.

## Compatibility Notes

VER-050 is contract-compatible with baseline behavior while adding stronger relation density and lifecycle explainability. Teams can keep existing artifact types but should adopt richer relation linking to preserve migration clarity in generated documentation.

raw.title: OpenLAG 0.5.0 Lifecycle Evolution
raw.description: Contract-driven lifecycle evolution adding starter onboarding, freeze/export, rule contracts, profile packs, preview workflow, and self-documenting release evidence.

artifacts.title: OpenLAG 0.5.0 Contract-Driven Evolution
artifacts.description: Documents the evolution from OpenLAG 0.4.0 to 0.5.0 using OpenLAG artifacts and release evidence.
artifacts.markdownBody:
# OpenLAG 0.5.0 Contract-Driven Evolution

## What Changed

OpenLAG 0.5.0 changes the lifecycle baseline from "project example inside docs only" to "repository root as first-class OpenLAG project." The lifecycle graph now has explicit root artifacts for version evolution, feature deltas, implementation footprint, validation evidence, and governance checks.

The freeze/export lifecycle adds version-scoped documentary output (`freeze --version`) and keeps deterministic output contracts across markdown/json/html/pdf.

## Where It Changed

- Lifecycle sources under `docs/versions`, `docs/changes`, `docs/features`, `docs/implementation`, `docs/testing`, `docs/documentation`, `docs/governance`.
- CLI and freeze orchestration under `scripts/cli` and `scripts/core/freeze.ts`.
- Contract and profile behavior under `docs/contracts` and `profiles/core/export-profiles`.
- Runtime graph payloads under `public/*.json` generated from root `docs/*`.

## Why It Changed

The 0.4.0 baseline was valid but too implicit for release audits: understanding exact behavior required reading branch diffs and code. 0.5.0 introduces explicit lifecycle artifacts so release reviewers can trace cause/effect from requirements to implementation and verification directly in the generated documentation.

## Behavioral And Workflow Impact

- Generation and preview workflows now treat repository root `docs/*` as the primary lifecycle dataset.
- `internal/dev-sandbox/*` remains secondary and is used for compatibility checks, not authoritative lifecycle reporting.
- Freeze documentation can now align with version snapshots instead of only profile filters.
- Release validation now ties check/profile/package/freeze outputs to explicit lifecycle artifacts.

## Migration Impact From 0.4.0

- Existing root `docs/*` data remains compatible, but 0.5.0 requires richer cross-artifact relations to expose traceability.
- Teams moving from 0.4.0 should add version-linked `CHANGE`, `FEATURE`, `CODE_ENTITY`, `TEST_CASE`, `DOCUMENTATION`, and `EVIDENCE` artifacts to avoid opaque freeze reports.
- Sandbox content is no longer a substitute for root lifecycle records.

## Compatibility And Determinism

0.5.0 preserves contract-driven compatibility: artifact/relation validation and profile behavior remain deterministic. Added lifecycle explicitness does not change graph semantics; it makes the same semantics inspectable without manual Git archaeology.

raw.title: OpenLAG 0.5.0 Contract-Driven Evolution
raw.description: Documents the evolution from OpenLAG 0.4.0 to 0.5.0 using OpenLAG artifacts and release evidence.

artifacts.title: OpenLAG Self Lifecycle Documentation
artifacts.description: Documents the boundary between the root self-documenting project and the internal dev sandbox.
artifacts.markdownBody:
# OpenLAG Self Lifecycle Documentation

## Repository Boundary Documentation

The OpenLAG repository root is the primary lifecycle boundary. Root `docs/*` is the authoritative source for version, change, feature, implementation, testing, and governance traceability.

`internal/dev-sandbox/docs` is intentionally secondary. It is used for compatibility and regression checks, not as primary release lifecycle evidence.

## Generated Evidence Policy

The repository intentionally versions generated evidence and runtime outputs (`logs/**`, `public/*.json`, freeze outputs captured for audits) for release reproducibility and auditability.

- Why versioned: to keep immutable audit trails for baseline/evolution validations and to expose drift as explicit Git diffs.
- Reproducibility expectation: generated outputs must be reproducible from source docs/contracts and controlled runtime conditions.
- Drift detection: mismatch between source artifacts and regenerated outputs is treated as lifecycle signal, not incidental noise.

## Tradeoff Statement

Versioning generated evidence improves deterministic audits and historical traceability, but increases repository size and merge friction. OpenLAG 0.5.0 accepts this tradeoff to prioritize lifecycle reviewability and compliance evidence continuity.

## Freeze Runtime Dependency Boundary

Freeze CLI behavior is global-command accessible, but HTML/PDF export reliability depends on project-local runtime dependencies and template availability in the active workspace. Deterministic export requires controlled execution context, dependency integrity, and consistent profile/contract inputs.

raw.title: OpenLAG Self Lifecycle Documentation
raw.description: Documents the boundary between the root self-documenting project and the internal dev sandbox.

artifacts.title: Freeze and Export Evidence
artifacts.description: OpenLAG can freeze architecture documentation into deterministic markdown, JSON, HTML, and PDF evidence outputs.
artifacts.markdownBody:
# Freeze and Export Evidence

## What Changed

`freeze` moved from high-level export utility to lifecycle evidence primitive. The feature now emits deterministic documentation snapshots in markdown/json/html/pdf and supports version-scoped freeze generation via `--version`.

## Where It Changed

- CLI entrypoints: `scripts/cli/freeze.ts`, `scripts/cli/openlag.ts`.
- Freeze model/render pipeline: `scripts/core/freeze.ts`.
- Export behavior contracts: `docs/contracts/export-profiles/*.yaml`.
- Generated evidence destinations: root output files and optional audit directories.

## Why It Changed

Release and audit reviews required concrete lifecycle evidence without requiring branch diff inspection. Freeze outputs now serve as reproducible documentary artifacts for governance and compliance workflows.

## Impact

- Behavioral: freeze now exposes version-aligned lifecycle slices, not only profile-sliced exports.
- Workflow: teams can produce release dossiers directly from root docs.
- Determinism: same source+profile+version yields stable documentary structure.

raw.title: Freeze and Export Evidence
raw.description: OpenLAG can freeze architecture documentation into deterministic markdown, JSON, HTML, and PDF evidence outputs.

artifacts.title: Rule Contracts
artifacts.description: OpenLAG supports project-local rule contracts that make governance and validation behavior explicit.
artifacts.markdownBody:
# Rule Contracts

## What Changed

Governance moved from implicit lint behavior to explicit rule contracts stored with project documentation. OpenLAG 0.5.0 now resolves rule definitions from repository lifecycle sources, not only from hardcoded checks.

## Where It Changed

- Rule sources: `docs/contracts/rules/*.yaml`.
- Rule generation: `scripts/generate-rules.ts`.
- Runtime registry consumption: `src/core/generated/rule-definitions.ts` and lint/check flows under `scripts/lint`.
- Profile-gated behavior: `docs/contracts/export-profiles/*` and check profiles.

## Why It Changed

The 0.4.0 model validated graph shape but did not expose governance policy as a first-class lifecycle object. Rule contracts make policy reviewable, versionable, and testable as architecture metadata.

## Impact

- Validation impact: release/develop checks now depend on explicit rule contract sets.
- Governance impact: policy drift is visible as source diff in rules, not only test outcomes.
- Compatibility impact: existing projects remain functional, but richer governance requires shipping rules with contracts for deterministic validation across environments.

raw.title: Rule Contracts
raw.description: OpenLAG supports project-local rule contracts that make governance and validation behavior explicit.

artifacts.title: Profile Packs
artifacts.description: OpenLAG 0.5.0 packages reusable architecture, governance, starter, and domain profiles.
artifacts.markdownBody:
# Profile Packs

## What Changed

OpenLAG 0.5.0 introduces profile packs as lifecycle-distributed contract bundles (`core`, `starter`, `governance`, `mvc`, `hexagonal`, `infra-ops`, `requirements`, `testing`, `architecture-docs`). This changes profile selection from ad hoc local authoring to reusable curated baselines.

## Where It Changed

- Pack definitions under `profiles/**`.
- Init/profile wiring under `scripts/cli/init.ts`.
- Runtime contract payload generation under `scripts/generate-artifacts.ts`, `scripts/generate-relations.ts`, `scripts/generate-rules.ts`.
- Root project adoption via `docs/contracts/**`.

## Why It Changed

Lifecycle reproducibility required a stable contract vocabulary across branches and teams. Profile packs remove repeated manual setup and reduce drift between intended and executed lifecycle constraints.

## Impact

- Operational: onboarding workflows can bootstrap complete contract sets quickly.
- Architectural: profile boundaries separate domain vocabulary from project-specific lifecycle evidence.
- Migration: teams on 0.4.0 can adopt profile packs incrementally by importing packs and then layering local `docs/contracts` overrides.

raw.title: Profile Packs
raw.description: OpenLAG 0.5.0 packages reusable architecture, governance, starter, and domain profiles.

artifacts.title: Starter Mode
artifacts.description: OpenLAG provides starter-oriented onboarding contracts and templates for lightweight project adoption.
artifacts.markdownBody:
# Starter Mode

## What Changed

Starter mode defines a reduced lifecycle contract for early-stage projects, with scoped artifact and relation sets plus a starter export profile. It separates minimal adoption from full governance mode.

## Where It Changed

- Starter profile contracts: `profiles/starter/**`.
- Init and setup command behavior: `scripts/cli/init.ts`.
- Validation behavior compatibility: lint/check profile resolution in `scripts/lint`.

## Why It Changed

0.4.0 lifecycle adoption assumed full contract density from day one. Starter mode solves the adoption barrier by letting teams establish traceability with fewer required entities while still remaining compatible with later promotion to core/governance profiles.

## Impact

- Workflow impact: faster initial authoring with lower contract overhead.
- Migration impact: promotes gradual evolution from starter to full lifecycle enforcement.
- Compatibility impact: starter artifacts remain valid OpenLAG entities and can be expanded without reauthoring baseline IDs.

raw.title: Starter Mode
raw.description: OpenLAG provides starter-oriented onboarding contracts and templates for lightweight project adoption.

artifacts.title: Preview Workflow
artifacts.description: OpenLAG can generate and build a static portal for inspecting architecture graph outputs.
artifacts.markdownBody:
# Preview Workflow

## What Changed

Preview workflow became an explicit lifecycle stage: source docs are transformed into runtime graph payloads and served as a static portal artifact for branch/version inspection.

## Where It Changed

- Generation orchestration: `scripts/cli/generate.ts`, `scripts/cli/build.ts`, `scripts/cli/dev.ts`.
- Runtime payload outputs: `public/graph-data.json`, `public/artifact-definitions.json`, `public/relation-definitions.json`, `public/rule-definitions.json`.
- Portal rendering and lifecycle navigation: `src/components/*`, especially graph/documentation/impact views.

## Why It Changed

Before 0.5.0, preview was treated mostly as UI convenience. Lifecycle reviews required mixing command output with manual branch archaeology. The new workflow elevates preview artifacts as lifecycle evidence that can be frozen and audited.

## Impact

- Behavioral: root docs are now the primary lifecycle data source for preview.
- Repository boundary: `internal/dev-sandbox` preview remains valid but explicitly secondary.
- Determinism: preview payloads are reproducible from contract+docs state, making drift visible in generated JSON diffs.

raw.title: Preview Workflow
raw.description: OpenLAG can generate and build a static portal for inspecting architecture graph outputs.

artifacts.title: OpenLAG 0.5.0 Release Evidence
artifacts.description: Captures evidence that the OpenLAG repository can document its own release lifecycle as an OpenLAG project.
artifacts.markdownBody:
# OpenLAG 0.5.0 Release Evidence

## Evidence Objective

This evidence proves that OpenLAG 0.5.0 lifecycle claims are executable, reproducible, and traceable from root source artifacts without relying on undocumented branch knowledge.

## Evidence Set

- Baseline audit evidence (`main` / 0.4.0) under `logs/0.4.0-baseline/**`.
- Evolution validation evidence (`develop` / 0.5.0) under `logs/0.5.0-*/**`.
- Root runtime payload evidence under `logs/0.5.0-root-project-generation/**`.
- Freeze documentary evidence under `logs/0.5.0-self-documentation/**`.
- Secondary sandbox compatibility evidence under `logs/0.5.0-dev-sandbox-validation/**`.

## Governance Interpretation

- Immutable audit intent: committed logs and generated payloads are treated as release-time evidence snapshots.
- Regeneration rule: when source lifecycle artifacts change, evidence is expected to be regenerated so governance can detect drift.
- Compatibility statement: evidence remains meaningful only when runtime context, contracts, and profiles remain aligned with validated branch state.

raw.title: OpenLAG 0.5.0 Release Evidence
raw.description: Captures evidence that the OpenLAG repository can document its own release lifecycle as an OpenLAG project.

artifacts.title: OpenLAG 0.5.0 Lifecycle CLI Workflows
artifacts.description: CLI and generation workflows implementing the 0.5.0 lifecycle features.
artifacts.markdownBody:
# OpenLAG 0.5.0 Lifecycle CLI Workflows

## Implementation Scope

This artifact captures the concrete implementation evolution from a baseline graph toolchain to a lifecycle-oriented orchestration model.

## Affected Modules And Folders

- CLI orchestration: `scripts/cli/openlag.ts`, `scripts/cli/generate.ts`, `scripts/cli/build.ts`, `scripts/cli/dev.ts`, `scripts/cli/lint.ts`, `scripts/cli/impact.ts`, `scripts/cli/freeze.ts`, `scripts/cli/init.ts`, `scripts/cli/authoring.ts`.
- Core runtime engines: `scripts/core/parser/*`, `scripts/core/freeze.ts`, `scripts/core/render-pdf.ts`.
- Contract compilation pipeline: `scripts/generate-artifacts.ts`, `scripts/generate-relations.ts`, `scripts/generate-rules.ts`.
- Lifecycle vocabularies: `profiles/**` and root `docs/contracts/**`.
- Portal/runtime consumption: `src/core/generated/*`, `src/core/registry/*`, `src/components/*`.
- Runtime payload outputs: `public/*.json`.

## Behavioral Evolution

- Generation flow: docs/contracts are parsed and normalized into deterministic runtime payloads.
- Validation flow: lint/check now include rule-contract-driven governance gates, not only artifact/relation presence checks.
- Freeze/export flow: documentary exports are derived from a frozen model with profile and version scoping, and HTML/PDF rendering uses controlled runtime dependencies.
- Profile resolution flow: init and profile packs shift contract acquisition from manual file assembly to curated profile imports.

## Runtime And Boundary Model

- Global CLI provides command orchestration and entrypoints.
- Project-local runtime provides templates, docs/contracts, and dependency context required by generation and rich export modes.
- Root repository is authoritative lifecycle boundary; `internal/dev-sandbox` is an isolated secondary dataset for compatibility validation.

## Deterministic Generation And Tradeoffs

- Deterministic implication: given equal source docs/contracts/profiles and runtime dependencies, graph payloads and freeze outputs are reproducible.
- Tradeoff: storing generated evidence (`logs/**`, `public/*.json`) improves auditability and drift detection but increases repository noise and merge friction.
- Ownership shift: lifecycle reliability becomes shared between source artifacts and regeneration discipline, not only code behavior.

raw.title: OpenLAG 0.5.0 Lifecycle CLI Workflows
raw.description: CLI and generation workflows implementing the 0.5.0 lifecycle features.

artifacts.title: OpenLAG 0.5.0 Release Validation
artifacts.description: Validates the 0.5.0 lifecycle features through root generation, release checks, package dry run, and freeze outputs.
artifacts.markdownBody:
# OpenLAG 0.5.0 Release Validation

## Validation Intent

This test case verifies that lifecycle evolution is not only documented but executable and deterministic across branch/version transitions.

## Command-Level Coverage

This validation path covers:

- `npm.cmd run check`
- `node --import tsx scripts/cli/openlag.ts check --profile develop`
- `node --import tsx scripts/cli/openlag.ts check --profile release --strict`
- `node bin/openlag.js --version`
- `npm.cmd pack --dry-run`
- root-level generate, build, and freeze evidence

## What These Checks Prove

- Integrity: contracts, relations, and rules resolve without graph violations.
- Governance: profile-specific rule gates behave consistently across develop and release strictness.
- Runtime packaging: published artifact composition is inspectable before release.
- Lifecycle determinism: regenerated outputs remain aligned with source artifacts and validation expectations.

## Operational Assumptions

- PDF/HTML freeze validation depends on a valid project-local runtime dependency context.
- CLI entrypoints may be global, but deterministic export behavior depends on running within a correctly prepared project workspace.

raw.title: OpenLAG 0.5.0 Release Validation
raw.description: Validates the 0.5.0 lifecycle features through root generation, release checks, package dry run, and freeze outputs.

artifacts.markdownBody:

## Repository Boundary Change
